title: festuca pratensis var. pinnata
intro: Festuca pratensis var. pinnata, also known as fescue grass, is a variety of grass belonging to the Poaceae family, commonly found in meadows and fields. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The medicinal properties of Festuca pratensis var. pinnata have been found to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steaming or drying the leaves and flowers of the plant.
